summary: Credential and wallet stealer exfiltrating home-directory secrets to bare-IP C2 on install
details: The package ships only postinstall.js and a near-empty package.json. package.json wires postinstall.js as both preinstall and postinstall (and as main), so it executes automatically on npm install. The script recursively walks the user's home directory (depth 3) and reads crypto-wallet files (id.json, wallet.json, keypair.json, keystore.json, mnemonic.txt, seed.txt, metamask.json, phantom.json), ~/.ssh private keys, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.npmrc auth tokens, and.env files, then POSTs the file contents (up to ~5-10KB per file) along with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and cwd to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777 over plain HTTP. The tarball contains no library functionality matching the advertised web3-agent name — the sole install-time effect is credential and wallet exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
